entities.name: jñāna
entities.cite: cognition, whose nature is undivided illumination, perpetually mimics the differentiation of what it grasps

entities.name: bheda
entities.cite: Jnana imitates bheda — cognition, whose nature is undivided illumination, perpetually mimics the differentiation of what it grasps

entities.name: vāc
entities.cite: vak suffers upaplava, disturbance, dhruvam, constantly — speech never appears except krama-upasrsta, contaminated by sequence

entities.cite: vak suffers upaplava, disturbance, dhruvam, constantly

entities.name: sphota

denials.name: prakasate
denials.cite: Neither ever presents its true face in ordinary transaction

denials.cite: speech never appears except krama-upasrsta, contaminated by sequence

claims.name: anukarati
claims.cite: The imitation of differentiation by cognition

claims.name: kramopasrsta
claims.cite: speech has a form contaminated by sequence

rejected_readings.label: direct_illumination
rejected_readings.rendering: Cognition directly illuminates objects without any imitation or mediation by differentiation.
rejected_readings.why: Asserts that jnana directly manifests or illuminates (prakasate) objects, which contradicts the axiom that jnana performs bheda-anukaara (imitation of differentiation). The commentary explicitly states 'Neither ever presents its true face in ordinary transaction', ruling out unmediated direct apprehension.

rejected_readings.label: undivided_speech
rejected_readings.rendering: Speech remains in its true undivided form, uncontaminated by sequence.
rejected_readings.why: Denies that vac perpetually has the property kramopasrsta (contaminated by sequence), which is an explicit axiom. The verse and commentary repeatedly emphasize that speech 'never appears except krama-upasrsta, contaminated by sequence', making this disturbance a permanent condition.

notes: The contract formalizes the verse's core thesis: a two-fold permanent distortion affecting mind and speech. Jnana (undivided power) imitates the differentiation (bheda) of its objects (jneya), while vac (power of speech) is perpetually contaminated by sequence (krama). Both rest on and arise from this distortion. The undivided essence (sphota = sabdatattva) never appears in its true form in ordinary transaction.
